danger_signals.visual_form: Tanks, battleships, airplanes, and artillery sprites rendered on-screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile forces; collision or projectile contact results in player death

danger_signals.visual_form: Bullets traveling across screen toward player biplane
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion; hitting player removes a life and reduces firepower

danger_signals.visual_form: Constantly moving background depicting war-torn landscape with Thai-inspired scenery
danger_signals.meaning: Player is in active combat zone; forward momentum is mandatory

danger_signals.signal: Increasing bullet speed in higher loops
danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile velocity visually accelerates as difficulty increases
danger_signals.meaning: Game state escalation; danger intensifies progressively

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated landing areas at end of stages where scrolling halts
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve from constant forward movement; stage checkpoint reached

safety_signals.visual_form: "B" icon items spawned from destroyed ground enemies; visual bomb counter
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive capability to clear screen of threats; stress relief mechanism

safety_signals.visual_form: "S" icons and point bonuses dropped by defeated enemy plane waves
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced firepower and resource accumulation; progression toward bonus lives

safety_signals.visual_form: Score multiplier visual feedback; bonus life awarded at 50000 points and intervals thereafter
safety_signals.meaning: Resource cushion against player death; continued survival capacity

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Runway landing zones designated as friendly territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tanks, battleships, airplanes, artillery as distinct enemy types
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Colored waves of enemy planes; ground-based vs. aerial variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: War-torn landscape with scattered military installations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Constant scrolling terrain with visible military vehicles and incoming projectiles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must maintain forward momentum while dodging and attacking; enemies actively fire at player position

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Runway appears on screen; scrolling halts; transition to next level scenery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can land safely; bomb stock multiplies by 3000 points; difficulty increases for subsequent stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bullet speed acceleration becomes perceptible; enemy density increases; visual complexity heightens
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy aircraft aim more accurately at player; second loop starts at stage 2 rather than stage 1; player firepower management becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player biplane sprite hit by projectile or collision with enemy vehicle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Life counter decreases; firepower resets to original state; respawn at last checkpoint reached

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thai-inspired scenery in backgrounds; scrolling left-right environmental variety; Apocalypse Now aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous lateral background movement designed to emotionally engage player with realistic war depiction

environmental_storytelling.example: Backgrounds depict Thai scenery and war-torn landscape with scrolling left-right movement to immerse player in military conflict setting inspired by Apocalypse Now

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type variety as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through stages features different military vehicle types (tanks, battleships, aircraft, artillery) indicating player advancement through distinct battle zones
Visual designation of safe landing zones at stage completion marks territorial control and progression through hostile airspace

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fires continuous shots up to screen height; deploys bombs with blast radius damage; can collect power-ups
Loses one life; firepower resets to base level; respawns at last checkpoint
Fires projectiles at player with increasing accuracy in higher loops; destroyed planes spawn "S" power-ups and point bonuses
Stationary or moving obstacles; destroyed units spawn "B" bomb icons; require player firepower or bombs to eliminate
Bullet speed increases noticeably; projectile density increases; aiming accuracy of enemy aircraft improves

notes: Sky Shark (Flying Shark in Japan) is a military-themed vertically scrolling shooter with minimal faction complexity but strong world-state communication through environmental design and difficulty escalation. The game deliberately uses Apocalypse Now aesthetic to convey war-zone immersion. Visual manifestation of danger is primarily through enemy vehicle density, projectile speed, and terrain scrolling. Safety is signaled through runway zones and collectible power-ups. The checkpoint system and firepower reset mechanic create visual/behavioral feedback for player damage state. No explicit political factions are present—combat is framed as generic military conflict with player as allied air force.
